0.title: Overview
0.text:
- Build name: Move-arang
- Main idea: Leveraged is your primary burst/cleanup weapon, while Boomerang provides repeated hits, procs, and area pressure
- Playstyle: keep moving, funnel enemies, use wall/area denial, and let boomerang hits/procs damage groups
- Strong for solo Nightmare because it has damage, sustain, mobility, and crowd control

1.title: Primary Weapon: Leveraged
1.text:
- Primary weapon: Leveraged
- Stat priority: max Damage and Draw Speed
- Put leftover points into Fire Rate
- Main use: one-shotting normal mobs and cleaning up enemies quickly
- Reload Speed is not important because this is mostly used for burst shots, not constant firing
- Key jokers: Eagle Lever, Headbang, Stacked Lever
- This weapon was not prestiged, so the setup should be easier to copy

2.title: Secondary Weapon: Boomerang
2.text:
- Secondary weapon: Boomerang
- Stat priority: max Damage and Lingering Time
- Why Damage: boosts every boomerang hit
- Why Lingering Time: keeps the boomerang active longer, giving more hits per throw
- Element: Fire
- Add some Lifesteal for sustain
- Key jokers: Lingering Throw, Explosive Rounds, Pickpick
- Explosive Rounds used: two Acid and one Fire
- Proc effects are strong because the boomerang hits many times per throw
- Pickpick may be harder to fit without prestige slots

3.title: Spells
3.text:
- Spells used: Boing, Wallow, and Ritual
- Main focus: use Wallow/wall for area denial
- Wall blocks paths, slows enemies, and forces mobs into predictable lines
- This makes enemies easier to hit with boomerang and Leveraged shots
- Ritual leaves healing around the map and can save you or teammates
- Spell cooldown is maxed so wall and utility spells are available more often

4.title: Character
4.text:
- Character stats: max Spell Cooldown and Movement Speed
- Why Spell Cooldown: keeps wall/utility spells available often
- Why Movement Speed: helps kiting, dodging, repositioning, and surviving big aggro pulls
- Character jokers: Extra Jump, Glass Cannon, Soul Siphon, Healthy Boy
- Extra Jump improves mobility and escape options
- Glass Cannon gives big damage
- Soul Siphon gives sustain from enemies and objects
- Healthy Boy adds survivability for Nightmare

5.title: Strategy and Run Tips
5.text:
- Use wall to deny space and force enemies into a single line
- Throw boomerang through grouped enemies for repeated hits and proc effects
- Use Leveraged to one-shot mobs and clean up priority targets
- Keep moving constantly: jump, dash, and reposition when overwhelmed
- Do not fight toward storms or bad terrain; check the map and reset your position
- Soul Siphon can turn enemy-spawning objects into easy healing sources
- When too many enemies are aggroed, focus on survival first, then funnel them and clear slowly
- For full clears, move through each POI and clear big question marks and soul houses
- Against Vulture: throw, turn, dash, jump, repeat
- Use wall to stop enemies chasing behind you during boss/objective fights
- If extraction gets messy, maintain movement, circle back for souls/healing, and prioritize getting out alive
